businessOverview: Standard Intelligence (si.inc) is a San Francisco AI research lab (legally Standard Intelligence PBC) building general-purpose foundation models for 'computer use' — agentic AI that operates software directly through its graphical interface the way a human does. Its first major model, FDM-1, is trained on an ~11-million-hour corpus of raw screen-recording video, an approach the team argues is the only way to scale 'action data' for general agents. The lab is famously tiny — roughly six people — and in April 2026 raised a $75M Series A led by Sequoia Capital and Spark Capital at a reported ~$500M valuation. It previously open-sourced 'hertz-dev' (Nov 2024), an 8.5B-parameter full-duplex conversational audio model, establishing a research track record before its computer-use work.
productModel: FDM-1 is a 'computer action model' that learns to operate software from video rather than from annotated screenshots or text. The team trained an inverse dynamics model (IDM, a masked-diffusion network) on ~40,000 hours of labeled footage to automatically infer keystrokes and mouse movements from screen changes, then used it to label the full ~11M-hour video corpus — moving computer use from a data-constrained to a compute-constrained regime. A custom video encoder is ~50–100x more token-efficient than prior state of the art, fitting roughly 1h40m of 30 FPS footage in a 1M-token context window. The forward dynamics model (FDM) predicts the next action directly from video and action tokens — no chain-of-thought, BPE, or tool use. Demonstrated capabilities include extruding a CAD gear in Blender, autonomously finding software bugs by exploring an app's state space, and learning to physically drive a Toyota RAV4 around a San Francisco block after ~1 hour of fine-tuning via a web control interface.
marketPositioning: Standard Intelligence is positioned as a 'neolab' (per The Information) — a new, research-first AI lab — pursuing computer-use/agentic AI through a contrarian video-pretraining thesis rather than the language-model-plus-screenshots approach used by incumbents. It competes conceptually with the computer-use efforts of the major labs: OpenAI (Operator / computer-using agent), Anthropic (Claude 'computer use'), and Google DeepMind (Project Mariner), as well as a wave of agent/RPA-automation startups. Its differentiation is architectural: training general intelligence 'in pixel space' on raw video to scale action data, which it claims yields more efficient, scalable computer control than screenshot- or text-token-based agents.
endMarkets: Agentic software automation / RPA replacement (autonomous GUI operation of enterprise apps)
CAD and engineering design workflows (e.g., Blender / CAD components)
Software QA and security testing (autonomous bug/vulnerability discovery via GUI exploration)
Robotics / embodied control (real-world car-driving demo via fine-tuning)
growthDrivers: Scaling video pre-training compute now that computer use is compute-constrained rather than data-constrained
Demand for agentic AI that can operate existing software end-to-end, displacing brittle RPA
Token-efficient video encoder (~50–100x) enabling long-context, hour-plus computer-use sequences
Generalization across domains (CAD, finance, engineering, ML research) from a single foundation model
Embodied/robotics extension demonstrated by rapid (~1hr) fine-tuning to drive a real car
Fresh $75M Series A capital and a top-tier investor/advisor network (Sequoia, Spark, Karpathy)

leadership.name: Galen Mead
leadership.title: Co-founder (CEO)
leadership.background: Co-founded Standard Intelligence at age ~21; met co-founder Devansh Pandey as a teenager through the Atlas Fellowship (2022). Left undergraduate studies to build the company. Credited contributor on hertz-dev and FDM-1.

leadership.name: Devansh Pandey
leadership.title: Co-founder
leadership.background: Co-founded Standard Intelligence at age ~20; met Galen Mead via the Atlas Fellowship (2022); left his undergraduate program to pursue the company's video-pretraining approach to general agents. Credited contributor on FDM-1 and hertz-dev.

leadership.name: Neel Redkar
leadership.title: Researcher / founding team member
leadership.background: Listed as a researcher on FDM-1 and credited on hertz-dev; part of the ~6-person San Francisco team.

leadership.name: Yudhister Kumar
leadership.title: Researcher / founding team member
leadership.background: Listed as a researcher on the FDM-1 model among the ~6-person Standard Intelligence team in San Francisco.

employeeCount: ~6 (six people at the time of its April 2026 Series A)
